journeySteps.title: Discover Amora
journeySteps.description: Learn about our vision, values, and regenerative approach to community development.
details: Learn about the land and location
Ask questions in a live Q&A

journeySteps.title: Request Investor Pack
journeySteps.description: Receive comprehensive investor materials including feasibility study, proformas, and development timeline.

journeySteps.title: Schedule Investment Call
journeySteps.description: Connect one-on-one with our team to discuss your investment goals and how they align with Amora.

journeySteps.title: Make Your Commitment
journeySteps.description: Choose your investment vehicle and formalize your contribution to the Amora vision.

journeySteps.title: Attend Community Call
journeySteps.description: Learn about the basics and ask any immediate questions about Amora.

journeySteps.title: Participate in Events
journeySteps.description: Join potlucks, events, workshops, and parties on our land to experience the community.

journeySteps.title: Community Training
journeySteps.description: Complete training in NVC (Nonviolent Communication), authentic relating, and other community practices.

journeySteps.title: Become an Amorian
journeySteps.description: Contemplate and sign our Love Letter, formally becoming a member of Amora 508c1a.

journeySteps.title: Participate in a Circle
journeySteps.description: Join one of our sociocratic circles to contribute to community decision-making.
details: Choose a circle aligned with your gifts

journeySteps.title: Explore Quests
journeySteps.description: Take on quests to contribute meaningfully to the village and demonstrate your commitment.

journeySteps.title: Co-Creator Right of Passage
journeySteps.description: Complete the right of passage with a vote from the Co-Creators circle to become a full member.

journeySteps.title: Explore & Apply for Roles
journeySteps.description: Find a role that aligns with your gifts and apply for the upcoming season.

journeySteps.title: Attend Community Call
journeySteps.description: Learn about the basics and ask any immediate questions about living at Amora.

journeySteps.title: Participate in Events
journeySteps.description: Join potlucks, events, workshops, and parties on our land to experience the community firsthand.

journeySteps.title: Explore Housing Options
journeySteps.description: Browse available lots and housing options to find your perfect spot in the village.

journeySteps.title: Become an Amorian
journeySteps.description: Contemplate and sign our Love Letter, formally becoming a member of Amora 508c1a.

journeySteps.title: Join the Waitlist
journeySteps.description: Get first right of refusal on land opportunities. Priority is first-come, first-served.

journeySteps.title: Resident Right of Passage
journeySteps.description: Complete the passage with a vote from current residents. Review the Good Neighbor criteria.

journeySteps.title: Purchase Land Share Agreement
journeySteps.description: Secure your land with a Land Share Agreement and required Land Credits.

journeySteps.title: Build Your Home
journeySteps.description: Work with our approved builders or bring your own plans.

journeySteps.title: Move In Celebration!
journeySteps.description: Welcome home! Celebrate with the Amorians and begin your life in the village.

journeySteps.title: Attend Community Call
journeySteps.description: Learn about business opportunities at Amora and ask questions about our economic model.

journeySteps.title: Explore Commercial Opportunities
journeySteps.description: Review available business opportunities and find one that excites you.

journeySteps.title: Become an Amorian
journeySteps.description: Contemplate and sign our Love Letter to formally join the Amora community.

journeySteps.title: Submit a Business Plan
journeySteps.description: Present your business concept for community review and approval.

journeySteps.title: Community Approval
journeySteps.description: Present your business to the Prosperity Circle for consent-based approval.

journeySteps.title: Sign Business Agreement
journeySteps.description: Formalize your business arrangement including revenue share and operational terms.

journeySteps.title: Launch Your Business
journeySteps.description: Open your doors and begin serving the Amora community and visitors!

circles.name: Permaculture Council
circles.subtitle: Land Stewardship & Food Systems
circles.description: Cares for the land through regenerative agriculture, landscaping, and ecological restoration.
circles.domain: Food systems, regenerative agriculture, landscaping, land stewardship
circles.members: Residents involved in farming, gardening, and land management

circles.name: Education Council
circles.subtitle: Learning & Development
circles.description: Supports children's education, adult learning, and knowledge sharing across the community.
circles.domain: Children's education, adult learning, knowledge sharing

circles.name: Culture & Arts Council
circles.subtitle: Creative Expression
circles.description: Cultivates artistic expression, musical events, and cultural programming for the community.
circles.domain: Musical events, artistic expression, cultural programming
circles.members: Artists, musicians, event organizers, cultural enthusiasts

circles.name: Health & Healing Council
circles.subtitle: Wellness & Care
circles.description: Coordinates wellness services, healing modalities, and community health initiatives.
circles.domain: Wellness services, healing modalities, community health
circles.members: Health practitioners, wellness facilitators, fitness leaders

circles.name: Building & Village Council
circles.subtitle: Infrastructure & Maintenance
circles.description: Oversees construction, maintenance, infrastructure development, and architectural decisions.
circles.members: Construction-savvy residents, architects, designers, maintenance coordinators

circles.name: Business & Finance Council
circles.subtitle: Economic Sustainability
circles.description: Governs community financial decisions, business enterprises, and economic sustainability.
circles.domain: Community financial governance, enterprises, financial sustainability, local economy

circles.name: Community Life Council
circles.subtitle: Social Connection
circles.description: Coordinates social events, conflict resolution, and community celebrations.

circles.name: Intergenerational Wisdom Council
circles.subtitle: Wisdom & Rights of Nature
circles.description: Bridges generations through elder care, children's advocacy, indigenous practices, and rights of nature.
circles.domain: Elder care, children's advocacy, indigenous practices, rights of nature
circles.members: Elders, parents, indigenous wisdom keepers, earth-centered practitioners

roles.name: Development Board of Directors
roles.purpose: Strategic oversight and fiduciary responsibility for business, legal, and financial aspects of creating Amora
roles.compensation: Equity participation in development profits and/or first right of refusal on premium lots
members: CEO and Founder (Jess or designated representative)
responsibilities: Approve major financial decisions and budget allocations
Oversee legal entity structure and compliance
Review and approve development timeline milestones
Ensure investor protection and return strategies
Approve lot sales pricing and terms
timeCommitment: Monthly Board Meetings (1-2 hours via video conference)

roles.name: Community Advisory Council
roles.purpose: Strategic input on community culture, programming, partnerships, and local integration
roles.compensation: First Right of Refusal on lot purchases, 50% Retreat Discounts, Token Allocation
responsibilities: Advise on community programming and cultural development
Provide introductions to potential residents and partners
Serve as ambassadors for the Amora vision
Ensure positive integration with local Costa Rican communities
timeCommitment: Bi-Monthly Virtual Advisory Sessions (1.5-2 hours, 6 per year)
Annual Visioning Retreat (2-3 days, all expenses covered)

roles.name: Leadership Council
roles.purpose: Coordinate between Circles and represent community interests to Core Team and Board
roles.compensation: Community recognition and leadership development opportunities
members: One Representative from each active Circle
responsibilities: Coordinate between Circles on overlapping issues
Represent community interests to Core Team and Board
timeCommitment: Annual Planning Retreat with Board and Advisory Council (2 days)

roles.name: Core Team
roles.purpose: Manages day-to-day project execution and serves as bridge between all governance levels
roles.terms: Ongoing during development, evolving as community matures
roles.compensation: Salary, equity participation, and community benefits
responsibilities: Day-to-day project execution and development coordination
Implement Board decisions and Circle initiatives
